questions.question: What are the two primary locations where a Zoom recording can be stored after a meeting?

answerOptions.rationale: Zoom provides flexibility by allowing users to save files directly to their hard drive or to a remote server for easier sharing.

answerOptions.text: Internal browser cache and USB drive
answerOptions.rationale: While data is temporarily cached during streaming, Zoom does not use the browser cache as a permanent storage solution for recorded sessions.

answerOptions.text: Cloud storage and social media platforms
answerOptions.rationale: Although meetings can be live-streamed to social media, they are not automatically archived there as a standard recording file.
Video files are generally too large to be stored as email attachments, though links to the cloud recording can be emailed.

questions.hint: Think about the difference between your own physical hardware and a remote online server.

questions.question: By default, which meeting participant has the authority to initiate a recording?

answerOptions.rationale: The host is granted administrative control to manage the meeting's privacy and data collection.

answerOptions.text: Any participant with a paid account
answerOptions.rationale: Subscription status does not automatically override the host's control over recording permissions during a live session.

answerOptions.text: The first person to join the meeting
answerOptions.rationale: Joining order does not determine administrative privileges, which are assigned to the meeting organizer.
While administrators set global policies, the individual host manages the recording for their specific meeting.

questions.hint: Consider who has the most control over the meeting settings and participant list.

questions.question: What is required for a non-host participant to record a Zoom meeting locally?

answerOptions.rationale: Host authorization is a security measure to ensure all parties are aware and consenting to the capture of the session.
Zoom's recording functionality is software-based and does not require external physical hardware beyond a standard computer.

answerOptions.text: An invitation link sent via text message
answerOptions.rationale: Meeting access methods do not influence the internal permissions required to trigger a recording.

answerOptions.text: A minimum of $5$ participants in the meeting
answerOptions.rationale: Recording capability is based on user roles and permissions rather than the total headcount of the meeting.

questions.hint: This involves a direct action taken by the person who organized the call.

questions.question: Which visual indicator signifies to all attendees that a Zoom session is currently being recorded?

answerOptions.text: A red icon in the top-left corner
answerOptions.rationale: This persistent visual cue ensures transparency so that all participants know their audio and video are being captured.

answerOptions.text: A yellow border around the shared screen
answerOptions.rationale: Yellow borders usually indicate that a specific window is being shared, not necessarily that it is being recorded.

answerOptions.text: A flashing green light on the participant list
answerOptions.rationale: Green icons or lights typically represent microphone activity or active speaker status rather than recording.

answerOptions.text: A pop-up message that stays in the center of the screen
answerOptions.rationale: While a notification appears at the start, a permanent central pop-up would obstruct the meeting view and is not used.

questions.hint: Look for a status change usually located at the edge of the interface.

questions.question: When a meeting is recorded to the cloud, which file type is typically generated to provide an audio-only version?

answerOptions.rationale: Zoom separates the audio stream into this compressed format to allow for easy distribution of podcasts or voice-only content.
While high quality, this format is uncompressed and much larger than the standards Zoom typically uses for cloud efficiency.
Though widely known, Zoom favors the M4A container for its specific audio encoding standards in cloud environments.
This lossless format is generally used for high-fidelity music and is not the standard for meeting audio capture.

questions.hint: This file extension is frequently associated with the MPEG-4 standard for audio.

questions.question: What feature of Zoom Cloud recording automatically converts spoken dialogue into a searchable text document?

answerOptions.rationale: This tool uses speech-to-text algorithms to create a written record that can be reviewed alongside the video.
Translation changes the language of the speech, whereas transcription simply converts speech into text in the same language.
Modulation alters the sound of the voice and is unrelated to creating text-based records of the conversation.
A summary provides a condensed overview, while a transcript provides the full word-for-word dialogue.

questions.hint: Think about the process of writing down exactly what is being said.

questions.question: What happens to a local recording if the host's computer loses power or shuts down unexpectedly during the meeting?

answerOptions.text: The file may become corrupted or fail to save
answerOptions.rationale: Local recordings require a graceful termination of the software to properly 'finalize' the video file on the hard drive.

answerOptions.text: The recording automatically moves to the Cloud
answerOptions.rationale: Local and Cloud recording paths are distinct; a local process cannot spontaneously switch to a cloud process mid-stream.

answerOptions.text: The recording continues through a participant's device
answerOptions.rationale: Recording is tied to the specific hardware that initiated it and does not jump between devices unless manually started elsewhere.

answerOptions.text: The file is saved to the Zoom website automatically
answerOptions.rationale: Local recordings are strictly saved to the local disk and are not synced to the web portal unless manually uploaded later.

questions.hint: Local files depend on the continuous operation of the software and hardware on which they are being created.

questions.question: Which specific benefit is unique to Cloud recording compared to Local recording?

answerOptions.rationale: The Cloud allows for different views, like Active Speaker and Gallery View, to be recorded as separate files for greater editing flexibility.
Recording to the cloud requires a constant connection to transmit the data to the remote server.
Noise suppression is a function of audio processing and is available regardless of where the file is stored.

answerOptions.text: Immediate access to the file without processing time
answerOptions.rationale: Cloud recordings actually require significant processing time on the server before they are ready for viewing.

questions.hint: Consider the processing power of a server versus a single user's view of the meeting.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'Automatic Recording' setting in Zoom?

answerOptions.text: To start the recording as soon as the meeting begins
answerOptions.rationale: This setting ensures that no part of a scheduled session is lost due to a manual oversight by the host.

answerOptions.text: To record only when the host is speaking
answerOptions.rationale: Automatic recording captures the entire session continuously, regardless of who is currently talking.

answerOptions.text: To stop the recording when the meeting reaches its scheduled end time
answerOptions.rationale: The recording typically stops only when the meeting is ended or the host manually intervenes, not just at the clock time.

answerOptions.text: To record only screen sharing segments
answerOptions.rationale: This setting records the entire meeting duration, including video feeds and screen sharing combined.

questions.hint: This feature removes the need for human intervention at the start of a session.

questions.question: To improve accessibility for viewers with hearing impairments, which Zoom Cloud feature should be prioritized?

answerOptions.rationale: Captions provide a synchronized text overlay of the audio, allowing users to follow the meeting without sound.
While HD improves clarity, it does not solve the fundamental accessibility barrier for those who cannot hear the audio.
Stereo audio improves sound depth but does not aid individuals who require visual alternatives to sound.
This setting disables echo cancellation for high-fidelity audio, which is unrelated to text-based accessibility.

questions.hint: This feature provides a text-based representation of the spoken word in real-time.
